title: Implementation of Honey Bee Routing Technique in Mobile Wireless Sensor Networks
author: Kundana, Konda
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Mobile Wireless Sensor Networks (MWSN) utilize transmitters to allow data gathering and transfer from stations to access points. Depending on the requirements of the application, these stations can be fixed or mobile. The deployment of mobile nodes that promote mobility and energy efficiency, and LEACH routing changes have produced a positive outcome. Communication throughout extended distances among base stations (BS) and cluster members can use a huge amount of energy in large networks. In order to provide reliable and energy-efficient navigation, a new routing method named Enhanced Routing Method for Enhanced Connectivity of Mobile Wireless Networks is introduced.   This study proposes a Low-Energy Adaptive Tier Clustering Hierarchy (LEATCH) method to find the best balance between latency and energy consumption to solve the MWSN coverage issues. Furthermore, a new Honey Bee algorithm is proposed with multi hop approach to select the optimal hyper cluster head and deploying the energy in a more efficient manner. In terms of network life, durability, coverage, stability and efficiency, the LEATCH protocol performs better than previous energy-saving protocols, which greatly benefits MWSN applications.

Depending on the requirements of the application, these stations can be fixed or mobile. Communication throughout extended distances among base stations (BS) and cluster members can use a huge amount of energy in large networks. This study proposes a Low-Energy Adaptive Tier Clustering Hierarchy (LEATCH) method to find the best balance between latency and energy consumption to solve the MWSN coverage issues.
